narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have the door close feels deeply personal, and I'm truly sorry you're facing this right now. Our prediction model looked at your profile and saw a 74.6% chance of struggling to pass the bar exam down the line, which is why we had to make this tough call on your application. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 2.0, ranking you near the bottom of your class academically during that critical later stage, and it was the strongest signal pulling toward this outcome. Layered on top was your Year 1 decile of 3.0, showing early grades that also landed in the lower third, reinforcing a pattern the model couldn't ignore. Your LSAT score of 38.0 added to that concern, as it signaled challenges with the kind of high-stakes reasoning the bar demands. Even with your upper-middle family income, which might offer some support, it didn't outweigh these academic hurdles in the model's view. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.0 fit into this picture too, suggesting a consistent thread of performance that worried us. As a white male pursuing full-time studies, those details nudged the prediction further toward risk, based on patterns we've seen in similar profiles. It's not about your potential or drive—it's this specific combination painting a picture of steeper odds ahead. Many students in your shoes have turned things around by targeting those early deciles with focused improvement plans before reapplying. You've got time to rewrite this story; consider bolstering those grades and LSAT prep to shift the narrative next time. We're rooting for you to prove this prediction wrong. Hang in there.
